The problem

You have AI tools.
They just don't know your work.

AI is everywhere. But your AI assistant doesn't know your team. Your note-taking app hasn't read yesterday's meeting. Your messenger's AI doesn't know who's blocked. The tools are smart, they just live outside your work.

🤖
AI Assistants
ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini
✕ Doesn't know your team or projects
✕ You explain context from scratch every time
✕ Can't take action inside your workspace
📝
Note-taking Apps
Notion, Confluence, Obsidian
✕ Only knows what someone remembered to write
✕ Decisions made in chat vanish without a trace
✕ No one updates the doc after the meeting
💬
Messengers with AI
Slack, Teams, Discord
✕ AI summarizes threads but can't act on them
✕ No one knows who owns what after the call
✕ Tasks only exist if someone manually creates them
🔧
Local AI Agents
OpenClaw, Local setups
✕ Requires installation, terminal, and config
✕ Only works for technical users who can set it up
✕ No team context, still just one person's AI
MARKHUB works instantly, no install, no code, no setup. Just sign up and start.
↓ scroll
Why MARKHUB

Not another Slack.
The layer above it.

MARKHUB isn't a messaging app. It's the execution layer that sits on top of every conversation, automatically turning what gets said into what gets done.

Features

Everything in one hub. Powered by MAKi.

MAKi is MARKHUB's built-in AI agent, not a chatbot you visit, but an AI that lives inside every hub and works alongside your team.

01 · Conversation Capture

Any conversation.
Auto-captured.

Chat thread, voice note, or live call. MARKHUB listens, transcribes, and extracts tasks in real time. No bots. No manual recap.

before you asked.

M
MAKi · Codex
auto-fix suggestion
Found the issue, the upload endpoint checks token expiry but doesn't refresh before the multipart request. Suggest adding a pre-request token refresh guard:
src/api/upload.ts line 47
async function uploadMedia(file: File) {
-  const token = getAuthToken();
+  const token = await refreshIfExpired(getAuthToken());
  return postMultipart('/upload', file, token);
}
Apply fix

FAQ

Common questions.

Is this just another Slack clone? +

No. Slack is a communication layer. MARKHUB is the execution layer that sits on top. In Slack, tasks only exist if someone manually creates them. In MARKHUB, the AI reads every conversation and creates them automatically.

How is this different from Otter or Fireflies? +

Otter and Fireflies record calls and give you a transcript. MARKHUB is a full workspace where conversation capture is just one channel. The output isn't a document, it's a task list ready to execute, inside the same app where your team works.

Does a bot join our calls? +

No bot joins. MARKHUB captures audio directly from your device, invisible to everyone else on the call. When the conversation ends, tasks appear in your Data Bucket automatically.

We already use Slack and Notion. Do we have to switch? +

MARKHUB can run alongside your existing tools. Most teams find they gradually stop needing them. MARKHUB already has chat, tasks, notes, calendar, and files in one place, with tasks that create themselves.

Is there a free plan? +

Yes. Free includes up to 5 members, 100 Chat AI extractions, and 5 Meeting AI calls per month. No credit card required. Pro ($19/mo) unlocks unlimited members, unlimited Chat AI, and 100 Meeting AI calls. Business ($99/mo) scales further with 300 calls, SSO, and priority support. Enterprise is custom-priced.

Start with MAKi
today.

No team needed. No setup. MAKi starts working the moment you do.

Common questions
What counts as a "Meeting AI" call?+
Any audio or video call processed through MARKHUB, phone calls via Markly, in-app group calls, or uploaded recordings. Each call counts as 1 regardless of duration. A 5-minute standup and a 90-minute investor call both use 1 credit.
Why is member count unlimited on paid plans?+
We think paying per seat is the wrong model for a tool built around team conversations. The more teammates you add, the more conversations happen, and the more value Meeting AI creates. You pay for AI usage, not headcount. Adding a new hire should feel free.
What is a "hub"?+
A hub is a self-contained workspace for a project, client, or team, chat, tasks, calendar, notes, and files all in one view. Think of it as a Slack channel that comes with a full project workspace built in. On Free you get 1 hub, Pro gives you 3, and Business unlocks unlimited.
Can I try before paying?+
Yes. The Free plan is free forever with no credit card required, 5 members, 1 hub, 5 Meeting AI calls per month. Pro and Business both include a 14-day free trial with full access. Cancel anytime, no questions asked.
What's the difference between Chat AI and Meeting AI?+
Chat AI reads your text conversations in real time and extracts tasks automatically, it's always on and unlimited on paid plans. Meeting AI is the heavy lifter: it transcribes recorded calls, generates summaries, and extracts structured tasks from spoken conversations. This is where the real time savings come from, and it's what we meter for billing.
What is the AI Agent and what can it do right now?+
The AI Agent (MAKi) currently handles task creation from conversations and in-hub research, finding references, summarizing links, and surfacing relevant context. Proactive execution (sending emails, wiring payments, booking meetings on your behalf) is in active development and will roll out to Pro and Business first.
Can I add more Meeting AI calls if I hit the limit?+
Yes. On Pro, additional calls are $0.50 each. On Business, $0.30 each. These are charged at the end of the billing cycle. There's no hard cutoff, calls keep working and overages appear on your next invoice. Enterprise plans include unlimited calls with no overage fees.
Is my conversation data used to train AI models?+
No. Your hub conversations, call transcriptions, and task data are never used to train AI models, ours or anyone else's. On Business and Enterprise plans, you can also opt for on-premise processing so data never leaves your infrastructure.

More from the Journal
Legal

Privacy Policy

Effective date: March 12, 2026  ·  Last updated: March 12, 2026

MARKHUB Inc. ("MARKHUB," "we," "our," or "us") is a Delaware corporation. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and protect your information when you use our platform, website, and related services (collectively, the "Services").

By using our Services, you agree to this Privacy Policy. If you do not agree, please discontinue use of the Services.

1. Information We Collect

We collect information you provide directly, information generated automatically through your use of the Services, and information from third parties:

  • Account information: name, email address, profile photo, and password when you register.
  • Conversation data: chat messages, call recordings, transcripts, and meeting notes you create or share within MARKHUB hubs.
  • Task and workspace data: tasks, tickets, comments, files, and other content you generate within the platform.
  • Usage data: pages visited, features used, device type, browser, IP address, and timestamps.
  • Payment information: billing name, address, and payment method details (processed by our third-party payment processor; we do not store full card numbers).
  • Communications: emails or messages you send to our support team.

2. How We Use Your Information

We use the information we collect to:

  • Provide, maintain, and improve the Services, including AI-powered task extraction and automation features.
  • Process transactions and send billing-related communications.
  • Authenticate your identity and maintain account security.
  • Send product updates, announcements, and support communications.
  • Analyze usage patterns to improve performance and user experience.
  • Comply with legal obligations and enforce our Terms of Service.
  • Prevent fraud, abuse, and security incidents.

We do not sell your personal information to third parties, and we do not use your conversation or task content to train our AI models without your explicit consent.

3. AI Processing

MARKHUB's core functionality involves AI analysis of conversations and calls to extract tasks and generate structured data. When you use these features, your conversation content is processed by our AI systems, which may include third-party AI infrastructure providers bound by confidentiality obligations. We process this data solely to provide the Services you have requested.

You may opt out of AI-powered features at any time through your account settings, in which case conversation content will not be processed by our AI systems.

4. Information Sharing and Disclosure

We share your information only in the following circumstances:

  • Service providers: trusted vendors who help us operate the platform (e.g., cloud hosting, payment processing, analytics), subject to data processing agreements.
  • Workspace members: content you create within a shared hub is visible to other members of that hub.
  • Business transfers: in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of assets, with prior notice to you.
  • Legal requirements: when required by law, subpoena, or other legal process, or to protect the rights and safety of MARKHUB, our users, or the public.
  • With your consent: in any other circumstances with your explicit consent.

5. Data Retention

We retain your data for as long as your account is active or as needed to provide the Services. Free plan accounts retain conversation history for 90 days; paid plans retain unlimited history unless you delete it. You may request deletion of your account and associated data at any time (see Section 8).

6. International Data Transfers

MARKHUB is headquartered in the United States. If you access the Services from outside the United States, your information may be transferred to, stored, and processed in the United States or other countries. If you are located in the European Economic Area (EEA), United Kingdom, or Switzerland, we rely on Standard Contractual Clauses approved by the European Commission as a legal mechanism for such transfers.

7. California Privacy Rights (CCPA/CPRA)

If you are a California resident, you have the following rights under the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and the California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA):

  • Right to Know: request disclosure of the categories and specific pieces of personal information we have collected about you.
  • Right to Delete: request deletion of personal information we have collected, subject to certain exceptions.
  • Right to Correct: request correction of inaccurate personal information.
  • Right to Opt-Out of Sale/Sharing: we do not sell or share personal information for cross-context behavioral advertising.
  • Right to Non-Discrimination: we will not discriminate against you for exercising your privacy rights.

To exercise these rights, contact us at [email protected]. We will respond within 45 days as required by law.

8. EEA / GDPR Rights

If you are located in the EEA, UK, or Switzerland, you have additional rights under the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR):

  • Right of access to your personal data.
  • Right to rectification of inaccurate data.
  • Right to erasure ("right to be forgotten").
  • Right to restriction of processing in certain circumstances.
  • Right to data portability in a machine-readable format.
  • Right to object to processing based on legitimate interests.
  • Right to withdraw consent at any time where processing is based on consent.

Our legal basis for processing is: (a) performance of a contract for core Services; (b) legitimate interests for security, fraud prevention, and product improvement; and (c) your consent for optional AI features and marketing. To exercise any of these rights, contact [email protected].

9. Cookies and Tracking

We use cookies and similar technologies to authenticate sessions, remember preferences, and analyze usage. You can control cookies through your browser settings. Disabling cookies may affect certain features of the Services. We do not use third-party advertising cookies.

10. Security

We implement industry-standard technical and organizational measures to protect your information, including encryption in transit (TLS) and at rest, access controls, and regular security reviews. No method of transmission over the internet is 100% secure, and we cannot guarantee absolute security.

11. Children's Privacy

The Services are not directed to individuals under 16 years of age. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 16. If we become aware that we have collected such information, we will delete it promptly.

12. Changes to This Policy

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of material changes by email or by a prominent notice within the Services at least 14 days before changes take effect. Continued use of the Services after the effective date constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

13. Contact Us
